steps.title: Find the intercept form
steps.content: For a graph of $y=f(x)$ or $y=g(x)$, the $y$-intercept occurs when $x=0$.

steps.title: Evaluate the displayed functions
steps.content:
For each displayed equation,
$$
a(2.3)^0+b=a+b
$$
So the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept is $a+b$. This expression depends on both constants together.

steps.title: Check what is shown directly
steps.content: The value $a+b$ is a sum, not a single constant or coefficient shown directly in either equation. Seeing $a$ and $b$ separately is not the same as seeing their sum. The answer is D.
